lines.text: (reading) Old Ideas. Old Culture. Old Customs. Old Habits.
These last two match. Both of them just say supplementary containment.
Random re-weigh this morning, folks. [pause] Compliance is a rhythm. Random means random.
(calling up) [warmly] Morning up there. Nice smooth lifts today, thank you.
[flatly] Copy.
(reading) Lot A. Declared three-eighty. Reads four hundred eleven and two.
(savoring) [slowly] Four hundred and eleven. Kilograms.
(then) People here still weigh in pounds. I did a year over in Rotterdam, you don't go back from that.
(reading) B, declared three-ninety, reads four twenty-one.
(then) C, three-seventy, reads four-oh-one.
(then) D, three-eighty, four eleven again.
[deliberate] Thirty kilos over. Times the four of them.
(then) That's a hundred and twenty kilos of something, Mr. Boone.
Could be the couch upgrade. Archival spec revision came through in March, they put mass into the—
[flatly] Re-run it. Cell check first.
(reading) Four eleven nine. Four-oh-eight two. Four thirteen five.
(reading) Calibration drift, cell three. All four reads invalidated. [pause] Corrected tare applied. Within tolerance after correction.
(reading) [flatly] Hm.
(to Mack) [quietly] Eleven years I've closed these clean.
(then) You keep a tidy load, Mr. Boone. They'd like you over there.
[flatly] Long as the paperwork's square.
(to the photographer) Get the D crate behind me. Light's better on the D.
